name: toggle-group
title: Toggle group
description: A set of related toggle buttons sharing one pressed state, built on Base UI's ToggleGroup and composing the Commons Toggle. Supports single-selection (at most one pressed) or multiple-selection modes, APG toolbar-style roving tabindex with RTL-aware arrow-key navigation, and a required accessible group name enforced by a dev guard. Horizontal or vertical orientation, controlled and uncontrolled.
useWhen: A small set of mutually related two-state presses (text alignment, view mode, map layers)
A segmented control where one option stays pressed (single selection)
A formatting toolbar where several options can be pressed at once (multiple selection)
avoidWhen: A single standalone press (use a Toggle)
Choosing exactly one option from a longer list submitted with a form (use a radio-group)
Selecting form values from a long list (use checkboxes)
Navigating between views or pages (use tabs or links)

keyboard: Tab moves focus into the group and lands on a single item (roving tabindex); Shift+Tab moves away from the whole group
Arrow keys move focus between items and are RTL-aware (in dir=rtl, ArrowLeft moves to the next item that sits visually to the left)
Enter or Space toggles the focused item
Focus loops from the last item back to the first by default (loopFocus)
Disabled items are skipped by arrow navigation
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (each Toggle item is at least 44x44px)
notes: The group renders role=group and MUST be named via aria-label or aria-labelledby; a dev-only guard warns when neither is present.
Each item is a Commons Toggle exposing aria-pressed, so pressed state is announced and shown by a non-color position/border cue that survives forced-colors mode.

Base UI reads direction from its DirectionProvider, not the DOM dir; an explicit dir prop is fed to the provider, otherwise AmbientDirection supplies the resolved DOM direction so arrow-key order matches the visual order in RTL.
Single vs multiple selection is controlled by the `multiple` prop; single selection keeps at most one item pressed.
